Incident Report — Confidential (Malaysia PDPA/CMSA context) — Seri Pelangi Renewables Berhad
Prepared by: Encik Azlan Firdhaus (Acting CISO) — contact: azlan.firdhaus@seripelangi.example — Date: 02 June 2026, 08:30 MYT
Summary: On 01 June 2026, our endpoint monitoring flagged a ransomware attempt affecting two file servers in the Shah Alam DC; partial encryption was contained, but potential data exfiltration occurred.
Initial forensics indicate copies of folders “ESG_DraftAssurance_Q2” and “DAX_Token_Pilot_Terms” were accessed by an external IP prior to containment.
The ESG draft shows a 14% negative variance in Scope 2 emissions versus March guidance; this information is non-public and may be price-sensitive under the CMSA materiality threshold.
The DAX Token Pilot terms include fee schedule, validator arrangements, and a tentative go‑live date for tokenised REC issuance on the Negarabit Digital Asset Exchange; these commercial terms are not yet announced.
As the Company is listed on Bursa Menara Berhad, we are assessing disclosure obligations against the Listing Requirements materiality test and timing for any necessary announcement.
An information barrier (Chinese Wall) has been instituted between the Digital Assets Unit and Group Treasury with immediate effect; Insider List (Senarai Orang Dalam) has been initiated and circulated to Compliance.
Insider List additions: Puan Norlina Bakar (Head of Compliance), Encik Haris Zikri (CIO), En. Zaid Omar (GM, Digital Assets); emails: compliance@seripelangi.example, haris.z@seripelangi.example, zaid.omar@seripelangi.example.
Trading blackout for Designated Persons is imposed from 02–15 June 2026; reminders issued re: no tipping, no selective disclosure, and pre-clearance of any urgent trades.
We have notified external counsel Rimba & Teh Partners and lender Nusantara Amanah Bank Berhad regarding potential implications for covenants under the Green Sukuk Facility term sheet (non-public, execution draft).
No customer data or NRIC numbers have been confirmed compromised; preliminary PDPA assessment categorises the risk as medium pending DLP log review and third‑party report from ForenSys Sdn Bhd.
For operations and awareness only, the monthly cyber hygiene webinar will discuss ransomware and crypto wallet safety as general education, not related to this incident nor any listing disclosure.
IT has rotated service credentials, disabled two admin accounts pending KYE revalidation, and commenced 24‑hour monitoring of outbound traffic to known C2 networks; a root cause report will follow within 5 business days.
Proposed next steps: complete SCADA network segmentation audit, validate ESG assurance workpapers against the GHG Protocol, and confirm DAX Pilot counterparty readiness under the revised go‑live timetable.
All external communications to route via Company Secretary, Cik Meera Thassan, at cosec@seripelangi.example; any Exchange queries to be logged in the Disclosure Register and approved prior to release.
